name: Brown-Rice Soup with Asparagus - Daley
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium saucepan, combine rice with 1 3/4 cups water. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer until the rice is tender and all the water has been absorbed, about 45 minutes.
2. Meanwhile, trim the woody ends from the asparagus and discard them. Bring a large pot of water to a boil. Add the asparagus and cook until it is tender-crisp, about 2 minutes. Drain and rinse under cold water. Cut into 1-inch pieces, reserving 1/4 cup tips for garnish. Set aside.
3. Coat the bottom of a large pot or Dutch oven with vegetable cooking spray. Add celery, onion, carrot, and thyme; cover and cook over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables are tender, about 10 minutes. Add broth and rice; bring to a boil and simmer for another 10 minutes.
4. Transfer the soup in small batches to a blender and blend until smooth. Return the blended soup to the pot. Stir in asparagus, green onions, parsley, soy sauce, hot pepper sauce, and pepper to taste. Return to a gentle simmer. Ladle into bowls and garnish with reserved asparagus tips and parsley sprigs. Makes 7 cups.
